notes: Alcohol can be brought into Japan within duty-free limits with taxes above the allowance
Food can be brought into Japan but strict rules apply to animal products and some fresh items
There is no limit on bringing cash into Japan, but amounts over ¥1,000,000 must be declared.
Duty-free allowances apply when entering Japan for alcohol, tobacco, and certain goods within set limits.
Personal electronics can be brought into Japan, but high-value goods may be subject to declaration.
Medication can be brought into Japan, but some drugs require approval or are restricted.
Tobacco can be brought into Japan within duty-free limits for personal use.
Weapons are generally prohibited from being brought into Japan without strict authorization.
Prescription drugs can be carried into Japan, but some require prior approval and documentation.
You must declare certain goods when entering Japan, especially items above duty-free limits or restricted goods.
